hypothesis: Implementing graph-size bucketing—forming batches from graphs within narrow node-count bins—under static-64 will reduce JAX recompilations by at least 90% and lower mean combined time per training step by at least 25% during the first 100,000 steps relative to unbucketed static-64 on QM9 and AFLOW at batch sizes 32 and 64, without a significant change in final test RMSE (p > 0.10) for SchNet, MPEU, and PaiNN.
